description: Does leased/sharecropped land qualify? Guidelines say 'landholding families' which implies ownership, not lease or sharecrop.
system_handling: Return AMBIGUOUS for land_ownership in [leases, sharecrop]

description: Cut-off date rule: new land purchases after 01.02.2019 do not qualify the transferee, but inheritance due to death of landowner DOES allow new eligibility.
system_handling: Flag as AMBIGUOUS — cannot determine from profile whether land was acquired before/after Feb 2019 or via inheritance

description: NE States exception: Assam, Meghalaya, J&K have alternate identification (community-based land certificates, Vanshavali lineage charts).
system_handling: Flag as AMBIGUOUS for state in [Assam, Meghalaya, Jammu & Kashmir, J&K]

description: SECC 2011 database is 15 years stale. Current household status (housing, income, deprivation) may differ significantly from 2011 data used for eligibility.
system_handling: Return AMBIGUOUS — cannot verify SECC listing from profile; use ration card as proxy

description: Several states extend Ayushman Bharat coverage beyond SECC to additional families. State-specific eligibility extensions vary.
system_handling: Return AMBIGUOUS for income between ₹1.2L-₹3L since state may cover them

description: Census towns vs gram panchayat areas: residents of census towns notified under panchayat may or may not qualify as 'rural' for MGNREGA.
system_handling: Flag is_urban=true as FAIL; is_urban=false as PASS; peri-urban as AMBIGUOUS

description: Migrant workers: which panchayat do they register in — origin or destination? Guidelines suggest origin but practice varies.
system_handling: Cannot determine from profile; flag occupation=Migrant Worker as AMBIGUOUS

description: Informal urban workers without documentation cannot prove occupational category membership for PM-JAY urban eligibility.
system_handling: Flag as AMBIGUOUS if occupation matches urban category but no supporting documents

description: 'Dilapidated house' not quantitatively defined in all states — local gram sabha verification determines this.
system_handling: Cannot determine from profile; flag land_ownership=owns with no housing data as AMBIGUOUS

description: No national property registry for cross-verification of pucca house ownership across states. Self-declaration is the only mechanism.
system_handling: land_ownership=owns marked AMBIGUOUS for PMAY-U (could be non-pucca); cannot cross-verify nationally

description: Unclear land title: beneficiary may need land allotted by gram panchayat. If they have no land at all, eligibility unclear.
system_handling: Return AMBIGUOUS for land_ownership=none — they may qualify if GP allots land

description: Income criteria: household income vs individual income unclear in some states. Annual income calculation methodology varies.
system_handling: Use annual_income as household income proxy; flag boundary cases (±20% of EWS/LIG/MIG thresholds) as AMBIGUOUS

description: States/UTs may set a cut-off date for urban residency. Migrants may not qualify if they haven't lived in the urban area long enough.
system_handling: Cannot determine from profile; flag as AMBIGUOUS for recent migrants

description: Multiple PMJDY accounts across banks during initial drive (2014). Technically only one PMJDY account allowed but duplicates exist.
system_handling: Flag has_bank_account=true as AMBIGUOUS rather than FAIL — may already have PMJDY

description: Proof requirements for 'poor household' vary by pathway — BPL ration card required for some, self-declaration sufficient for migrants.
system_handling: Accept ration card (AAY/PHH) OR low income as proxy for poor household

description: Household definition for migrant families — 'no LPG in household' but migrant's origin family may have one.
system_handling: Cannot verify from profile; flag migrant workers as AMBIGUOUS

description: State BPL lists vary in criteria, methodology, and freshness. NSAP uses state BPL lists which may differ from SECC or central BPL criteria.
system_handling: Use has_ration_card in [AAY, PHH] as proxy for BPL status; flag 'unknown' as AMBIGUOUS

description: State top-up amounts vary widely — from ₹0 to ₹2,500+ per month depending on state. Central amount is the floor.
system_handling: Report central contribution only; note state top-up varies. Not an eligibility ambiguity.

description: State-level interpretation of 'no other pension' varies. Some states exclude family support, others only formal pension.
system_handling: Flag is_govt_employee=true as FAIL; otherwise AMBIGUOUS on pension from other sources

description: Pension on remarriage: central IGNWPS guidelines clearly revoke pension on remarriage. State implementation may vary.
system_handling: Central rule is clear — marital_status=married after widowhood = FAIL
resolution: PARTIALLY RESOLVED — central IGNWPS explicitly revokes pension on remarriage

description: Age range for IGNWPS: central guidelines say 40-79 (with 80+ getting higher rate). Some states have different age entry points.
system_handling: Use central rule: age >= 40 for eligibility

description: Disability 40-79% excluded from central IGNDPS (requires 80%+) but some states run separate schemes for 40-79% disabled.
system_handling: Central rule: disability_percent < 80 = FAIL for IGNDPS. Mention state schemes may cover 40-79%.

description: Dual NSAP eligibility: a disabled widow could theoretically qualify for both IGNDPS and IGNWPS. No clear central guideline on whether both can be claimed.
system_handling: Flag as AMBIGUOUS; show both as potentially eligible with note

description: Informal workers with brief formal stints may have been EPF members technically but are now informal. EPF membership status may be stale.
system_handling: Flag is_epf_member as AMBIGUOUS if occupation suggests informal worker with prior formal history

description: Income verification for informal workers is difficult — no payslip or ITR. Self-declaration basis.
system_handling: Accept self-declared annual_income; flag boundary cases (₹1.6L-₹1.8L) as AMBIGUOUS
source: PM-SYM Brief from Ministry of Labour and Employment

description: Vendors without any documentation need Letter of Recommendation from ULB. ULB discretion applies — LoR not guaranteed.
system_handling: Flag occupation=Street Vendor without documents as AMBIGUOUS (LoR is pathway but not guaranteed)

description: Previously believed there was a March 2020 cut-off for vendor identification. This is incorrect.
system_handling: No cut-off date. New vendors eligible via Category C/D LoR route.
resolution: RESOLVED — PM-SVANidhi has no March 2020 cut-off; new vendors eligible via LoR

description: Activities 'allied to agriculture' boundary unclear — specifically poultry, dairy, beekeeping qualify; core crop farming does NOT.
system_handling: Flag occupation=Farmer/Agricultural Labourer as AMBIGUOUS; allied activities as PASS

description: Bank may require skills/experience/knowledge for proposed activity. Requirement is discretionary at bank level.
system_handling: Cannot determine from profile; note as bank-discretionary requirement

description: One per family rule: 'family' defined as self and spouse. Multiple adult children starting separate enterprises — unclear if each counts as separate family.
system_handling: Flag previous_scheme_loans containing pmegp as AMBIGUOUS rather than definitive FAIL

description: If first enterprise was closed/wound up, is a second enterprise still 'greenfield'? Not explicitly addressed.
system_handling: Flag has_existing_enterprise=true as FAIL; closed enterprise as AMBIGUOUS

description: For non-individual enterprises (companies), 51% shareholding by SC/ST or woman required. Cannot verify from profile.
system_handling: For individual applicants, check caste/gender directly. Corporate structure requires manual verification.

description: Blended families (step-children, adopted children) — one account per girl child but family limit of 2 may cause issues.
system_handling: Flag previous_scheme_loans containing sukanya_samriddhi as AMBIGUOUS for blended families

description: Whether miscarriage/stillbirth counts as 'first birth' for PMMVY. Previously unclear.
system_handling: Miscarriage/stillbirth treated as fresh case — does NOT count as first birth.
resolution: RESOLVED — PMMVY 2.0 guidelines treat miscarriage/stillbirth as fresh case for future pregnancy

description: Twins/triplets in second pregnancy — if one or more are girls, incentive applies per PMMVY 2.0 norms.
system_handling: Flag is_pregnant_or_lactating=true with num_children>=2 as AMBIGUOUS — may qualify for second child incentive

description: AAY identification criteria vary by state — state determines who qualifies as 'poorest of poor' for AAY vs PHH.
system_handling: Cannot determine AAY vs PHH from profile; use income and vulnerability proxies; flag as AMBIGUOUS

description: PHH inclusion/exclusion criteria entirely state-specific. Central NFSA sets coverage percentage (75% rural, 50% urban) but states choose who is included.
system_handling: Use income proxy and vulnerability proxy as signals; always flag as AMBIGUOUS due to state variation

description: One Nation One Ration Card (ONORC): portability across states enabled but implementation varies. Migrant workers may face access issues.
system_handling: Note ONORC availability; flag migrant workers as AMBIGUOUS for ration card portability

description: Head of family on ration card must be eldest woman member (18+). Male only if no adult woman. This affects who can apply.
system_handling: Note in results that female head of family preferred for ration card application

description: Part-time artisan who also has another job — does 'on self-employment basis' exclude them?
system_handling: Flag occupation ambiguously matching traditional trade as AMBIGUOUS if also shows formal employment indicators

description: 'Availed' vs 'outstanding': no-recent-credit-scheme rule uses 'availed' not 'outstanding' — fully repaid loans still count as disqualifying.
system_handling: Flag previous_scheme_loans containing pmegp/pm_svanidhi/pm_mudra as AMBIGUOUS with note that repaid loans may still disqualify

description: Gram panchayat/ULB verification step for PM Vishwakarma registration may have local backlogs or varying criteria.
system_handling: Cannot determine from profile; note verification step required

description: States can redefine EWS income criteria with MoHUA concurrence — EWS income ceiling of ₹3L is central default but states may change it.
system_handling: Use ₹3L/₹6L/₹9L as central defaults; flag boundary cases as AMBIGUOUS

description: IGNOAPS requires 'no other pension' — unclear whether family support (children sending money) counts as pension/financial assistance.
system_handling: Only formal pension/govt financial assistance counts; family support does not disqualify

description: PM-KISAN excludes government employees but explicitly carves out Class IV / Multi-Tasking Staff / Group D workers (peons, sweepers, gardeners). The engine cannot distinguish Group D from other government employees — both is_govt_employee=true cases map to FAIL.
system_handling: Flag is_govt_employee=true as FAIL with note that Group D employees may still qualify. User must self-verify at pmkisan.gov.in.
source: PM-KISAN Revised Operational Guidelines dated 21.06.2019, Clause 3.3
